worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Simon Willison found Opus 4.7 uses 1.46x tokens for text and up to 3x for images vs 4.6 at the same per-token price — a real hidden cost anyone building on the API needs to know about.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Important correction to the above: the 3x image cost only applies to high-res images, not standard ones, so the actual pricing impact depends heavily on your use case.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Concrete user report of Opus 4.7 confidently sticking to wrong answers and forcing a rollback to 4.6 — relevant for anyone deciding whether to trust 4.7 in production agents.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Claude Code reverse-engineered into a visual map of 512K lines and 1,900 files — useful for anyone trying to understand or extend the agent loop and tooling.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Claude Code can now run free against open models (GLM 5.1, Gemma 4) — lowers the floor for indie devs experimenting with agentic coding without paying Anthropic.

worthClicking.author: @kimmonismus
worthClicking.why: NSA is using Anthropic's Mythos Preview despite labeling Anthropic a 'supply chain risk' — a telling data point on government AI adoption and vendor trust tensions.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Mollick argues o1-preview was the second most important LLM release ever and OpenAI gave away a massive strategic advantage by publishing it — good framing for thinking about openness vs. moat.

worthClicking.author: @DataChaz
worthClicking.why: Anthropic published a 33-page official guide on Claude Skills — actual blueprint worth reading if you're building Claude-native products.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: Sharp one-liner that captured 81K views: in the AI era, any roadmap beyond 30 days is dead weight — directly relevant to AI product strategy.

voices.why: Doing actual empirical work on Opus 4.7 token costs with a real tool — the kind of grounded analysis that cuts through hype.

Consistently sharp on distribution, product strategy, and agent-native thinking; the 30-day roadmap post and the 'systems must be reinvented for agents' thread are both on-point for the operator's interests.
Thoughtful on AI industry dynamics and strategic decisions by labs — the o1-preview framing is the kind of perspective useful for product positioning.

examples: @aakashgupta bulk-posting off-topic trivia (tennis longevity, NASA splashdown, Nolan film, Wolf of Wall Street, Ariana Grande)
@RoundtableSpace crypto pump posts (AAVE exploit, Polymarket trading bots, 'GM CT' engagement bait)
travel diary and motivational quote filler (@kimmonismus China flight, @DataChaz pressure quotes)
